items.description: These Regulations amend the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (S.S.I. 2023/242) to correct minor errors in the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023. Regulation 2(13) makes provision for instances in which a remedy member’s legacy scheme is the 2007 scheme. This aligns with the provision already set out in regulation 51 of the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 in relation to members whose legacy scheme is the 1992 scheme.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”). They correct minor errors in the 2023 Regulations. Regulation 10 makes new provision for a member who would have been able to transfer in service to the scheme established by the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 or the Police Pensions (Scotland) Regulations 2007 during the relevant period. Such service may now be transferred in provided certain conditions are met.

items.title: The Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force various provisions of the Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The Social Security (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 and Saving and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring sections 2, 9, 17, 20, 21, 22, and 23 of the Social Security (Scotland) (Amendment) Act 2025 (“the Act”) into force on 10 May 2025.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends schedule 1 of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2013, which by virtue of article 3(1)(a) of that Order contains a list of proceedings in relation to which the application of section 4(1) of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 is excluded. The effect is that evidence of previous convictions which have become “spent” under that Act is nonetheless admissible in those proceedings. This Order adds to the list in schedule 1 proceedings relating to sexual harm prevention orders and sexual risk orders under the Abusive Behaviour and Sexual Harm (Scotland) Act 2016.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations correct minor errors in the Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (S.S.I. 2023/241).

items.title: The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Thurso and Scrabster) (20mph, 30mph and 40mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Golspie) (20mph, 30mph and 40mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of fees to the Scottish Ministers for appeals made under the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 and by virtue of the Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) (Scotland) Regulations 1984. These Regulations apply to the appeals listed in regulation 1(2) The level of fee is to be calculated in accordance with the schedule.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Fees Regulations”) and the Town and Country Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 2015.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Local Reviews) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of fees to a planning authority in respect of local reviews conducted by virtue of section 43A(8)(a) or (b) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997. The level of fee is to be calculated in accordance with the schedule.

The Education (Fees and Student Support) (Miscellaneous Amendment and Revocation) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Fees Regulations”) and the Student Support (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Student Support Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Local Services Franchises Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These regulations make provision about the registration and the variation or cancellation of registrations of services during the transition of a local bus market into, and out of, a franchising framework made by a local transport authority under the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”). They make provision about registrations, and variations and cancellations of registrations when a franchising framework has been made but before sections 6 to 9 of the Transport Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) cease to have effect in relation to services included in the franchising framework. They also make provision which enables services which could not otherwise be registered to be registered, varied or cancelled, and provide that the relevant notice period in relation to such services expires when a service is no longer included in the franchising framework.

items.title: The Human Tissue (Supply of Information about Transplants) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations specify information that relevant clinicians must provide to the Human Tissue Authority in connection with organ transplants.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints the judges who may hear appeals under section 7 of the Valuation of Lands (Scotland) Amendment Act 1879. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2023 which previously made such provision.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints three judges to hear registration appeals under section 57 of the Representation of the People Act 1983. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2021 which previously made such provision.

items.title: The Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002 Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends Part 2 of schedule 2 of the Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002. Schedule 2 of that Act specifies the persons liable to investigation by the Scottish Public Services Ombudsman. The Order adds Quality Meat Scotland and the Scottish Pubs Code Adjudicator to the list of persons specified for this purpose. The Order also removes five bodies, which no longer exist, from the schedule.

items.title: The Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend existing provisions regarding the sharing of information in connection with the social security functions of the Scottish Ministers that are contained in the Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Appeal Committee Procedures) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Appeal Committee Procedures) (Scotland) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”).

items.title: The Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (Consequential Amendments and Forms) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments and prescribe forms in consequence of provision made by the Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”).

items.title: The Valuation (Proposals Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the making of proposals by proprietors, tenants and occupiers of lands and heritages for alteration of entries in the valuation roll for non-domestic rates.

items.title: The Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the Principal Regulations”) which make provision as to the membership and procedure of Health Boards established under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the 1978 Act”). Appointments to the Health Boards are made by the Scottish Ministers.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Highland Council) Designation Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Highland Council) Designation Order 2016 (“the 2016 Order”).

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 to provide for new member contribution rates, with effect from 1 July 2025.

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 3) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”). These Regulations come into force on 1 August 2025.

items.title: The Teachers’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Amendment) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Teachers’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2014, which set out the Scottish Teachers’ Pension Scheme 2015, to clarify and add to those categories of eligible employment for the purposes of that scheme which are specified in schedule 1 of those regulations. In particular, music instructors, educational psychologists, quality improvement managers, and certain other employments relating to educational research and the management of teachers, are added to the categories of eligible employment.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Crieff) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Seed (Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seed (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (“the Principal Regulations”), which prescribe fees for matters relating to:

items.title: The Marketing of Fruit Plant and Propagating Material (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These regulations amend schedules 6, 6A and 7 of the Marketing of Fruit Plant and Propagating Material (Scotland) Regulations 2017 (‘the 2017 Regulations’), which specify Regulated Non-Quarantine Pests (‘RNQPs’) and associated requirements regarding their plant hosts. RNQPs are pests that are present in Scotland and regulated in relation to their plant hosts.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Children (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 3 and Saving Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: The Children (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Act”) received Royal Assent on 1 October 2020. Sections 32, 33 and 35 came into force the following day.

items.title: The Land and Buildings Transaction Tax (Group Relief and Sub-sale Development Relief Modifications) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order modifies the Land and Buildings Transaction Tax (Scotland) Act 2013. It modifies paragraph 7 of schedule 10 and paragraph 4 of schedule 10A which provide certain reliefs from Land and Buildings Transaction Tax.

items.title: The M90/A90 Trunk Road (Junction 10 Craigend Interchange Southbound) (Temporary Vehicle Weight Restriction) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The A835 Trunk Road (Ardcharnich) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2025

The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The Environmental Authorisations (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Environmental Authorisations (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (the “2018 Regulations”) to bring water, waste and industrial emissions activities into the framework for authorisation of environmental activities provided by the 2018 Regulations, which currently only apply to radioactive substances activities in Scotland.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2025 (“the principal instrument”).

items.title: The Agriculture and Rural Communities (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 16 June 2025 the substantive provisions of the Agriculture and Rural Communities (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”) so far as not already in force.

items.title: The Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (Commencement No. 2, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring various provisions of the Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the Act”) into force. The Bill for the Act received Royal Assent on 9 August 2023. By virtue of section 21(1), that section, together with sections 20 and 22, came into force on 10 August 2023.

items.title: The Prisoners (Early Release) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 11 February 2025 all of the provisions of the Prisoners (Early Release) (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the 2025 Act”), so far as not already in force.

items.title: The Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring provisions of the Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) into force.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) (Amendment) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2025 (“2025 instrument”), which is not yet in force. Paragraph 2(2) corrects a typographical error in paragraph 2(3)(f) of the 2025 instrument. Paragraph 2(3) corrects an error in the new rule 62.28(3AAA) of the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443) which is to be inserted by the 2025 instrument. This instrument substitutes a new rule 62.28(3AAA) for the one to be inserted by the 2025 instrument to correct the error.

The Police (Ethics, Conduct and Scrutiny) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 25 June 2025 sections 1 to 9, 12, 13, 16, 19 and 20 of the Police (Ethics, Conduct and Scrutiny) (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”). Section 9 of the Act, which inserts section 59A (Scottish police advisory list and Scottish police barred list) into the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012, is commenced only for the purpose of making regulations under section 59A(6) and (7).

items.title: The Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2019 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for the control and eradication of bovine viral diarrhoea (“BVD”) in Scotland. The amendments made by article 2 of the Order come into force on 1 July 2025, and the amendments made by article 3 of the Order come into force on 1 February 2026.

items.title: The Regional Strategic Bodies and Regional Colleges (Glasgow and Lanarkshire) Order 2025
items.description: This Order abolishes two regional strategic bodies and designates four regional colleges under the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”).

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Fort Augustus) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The Vulnerable Witnesses (Criminal Evidence) (Scotland) Act 2019 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 27 June 2025 sections 6 and 10(4) of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Criminal Evidence) (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Scottish Road Works Register (Prescribed Fees) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 112A(1) of the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (“the Act”) provides for the Scottish Road Works Commissioner (“the Commissioner”) to keep a register to be known as the Scottish Road Works Register (“the SRWR”).

The Glasgow City Council Govan Partick Opening Bridge (Variation) Scheme 2024 Confirmation Instrument 2025
The A82 Trunk Road (Inverness) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) Order 2007. It updates the marine area for which local authorities are designated as planning authority in respect of marine fish farm development so that these areas extend to the extent of the Scottish marine area.

items.title: The Restitution Fund (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: Where a restitution order is imposed on an individual under section 253A of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (“the 1995 Act”), all monies received by the court in payment of the order are to be paid to the Scottish Ministers under section 253B(1). The Scottish Ministers are then obliged under section 253B(2) to pay all monies so received into a fund to be known as the Restitution Fund (“the Fund”). The Restitution Fund (Scotland) Order 2021 (“the 2021 Order”) provided for the delegation of the establishment, maintenance and administration of the Fund to the Scottish Police Benevolent Fund.

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Knockcushan Street) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A82 Trunk Road (Balchraggan) (Temporary Clearway) Order 2025
The Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations correct minor errors in the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”), including in respect of some amendments to the 2023 Regulations made by the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025.

items.title: The Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Regulations 2020 (“the 2020 Regulations”) which make provision for the operation of a deposit and return scheme. These Regulations come into force on the day after they are made.

items.title: The Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland (Designation of Scheme Administrator) Order 2025
items.description: This Order designates the scheme administrator which will operate and manage the deposit and return scheme (“the Deposit and Return Scheme”) established under the Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Regulations 2020. The Deposit and Return Scheme operates in relation to scheme articles. Scheme articles are drinks that are intended to be sold to consumers in Scotland and are contained in single-use packaging made from polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic, aluminium and steel. The Deposit and Return Scheme also applies to scheme packaging, being the packaging for those scheme articles. The Order comes into force on the day after it is made.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Order 2005 (“the principal Order”).

The Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations correct minor errors in the Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”), in particular, in relation to the definitions of “election” and “eligible decision-maker”.

The Environmental Regulation (Enforcement Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Environmental Regulation (Enforcement Measures) (Scotland) Order 2015 (“the 2015 Order”) so as to specify offences under the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging and Packaging Waste) Regulations 2024 as relevant offences under the 2015 Order and to enable the Scottish Environment Protection Agency to impose civil enforcement measures on persons in relation to those offences.

items.title: The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 4) Regulations 2025
items.description: Regulation 2 and the schedule of these Regulations bring into force the provisions of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) specified in column 1 of the table in the schedule on 3 March 2025. If a purpose is stated in column 3 of the table in the schedule, the provision is only brought into force for that purpose. These provisions relate to the register of accredited bodies and registration in that register and will enable applications under section 47(1)(a) of the Disclosure Act to be made and processed by the Scottish Ministers from 3 March 2025.

The Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the amount of NHS charges which can be recovered from a person who pays compensation to an injured person where that injured person has received National Health Service treatment or ambulance services.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Offshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order prohibits specified methods of fishing within specified areas.

items.title: The Plant Health (Export Certification) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order is made under sections 3(1) and 4A of the Plant Health Act 1967 and makes provision in the area of plant health fees.

items.title: The Home Detention Curfew (Amendment of Specified Time Periods) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: Section 3AA(1) of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) provides Scottish Ministers with the power to release a prisoner on licence. Such a release requires to be subject to a curfew condition (and is commonly known as a release on home detention curfew) (section 12AA of the 1993 Act). Section 3AA(2) provides that Scottish Ministers cannot exercise that power before the prisoner has served one quarter of their sentence. Subsection (3) provides that a short-term prisoner may be released on licence at any point in the period which begins 180 days (i.e. 166 days plus 14 days) before they are otherwise entitled to be released under Part 1 of that Act and ends 14 days before that point is reached. A long-term prisoner may be released on licence during the period which begins 180 days before they have served one-half of their sentence and ends 14 days before that point is reached.

items.title: The Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) on 16 September 2025. With the exception of section 61, the relevant provisions being commenced on that date contain Ministerial powers to make subordinate legislation. Section 61 (Regulations) specifies the Parliamentary procedure applicable to regulations made under certain provisions of the Act.

items.title: The National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“2024 Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the common staffing method. The common staffing method was introduced into the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“1978 Act”) by section 4 of the Health and Care (Staffing) (Scotland) Act 2019 and includes, among other things, the use of a “staffing level tool” and a “professional judgement tool” in determining the appropriate staffing levels for particular kinds of health care provision.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the Principal Regulations”) which regulate the system of rally control on public roads in Scotland. The Regulations provide a new fee structure for rallies and make minor amendments to update various references.

items.title: The Caledonian Maritime Assets (Port Ellen Works) Harbour Revision Order 2025
items.description: This Order, made on the application of the Caledonian Maritime Assets Limited, empowers CMAL to construct, maintain and operate in the Isle of Islay and on land at Port Ellen, harbour works and harbour related facilities at the harbour.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 2) Order 2025

The Council Tax (Dwellings and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax (Dwellings and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Fees for Scheme Membership and Disclosure Applications (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision under sections 70(1) and (2) of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”) for the fees payable in respect of applications to join the PVG Scheme and for confirmation of scheme membership. They also make provision under section 61(1), (2)(a) and (b) and (3) of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) in respect of applications for Level 1 and Level 2 disclosures.

items.title: The Social Security (Residence and Presence Requirements) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the social security assistance entitlement of certain persons arriving in Scotland from Israel, the Occupied Palestinian Territories (the West Bank, including East Jerusalem, and Gaza) or the occupied Golan Heights following the escalation of violence between Israel and Iran that began on 13 June 2025. These Regulations also make provision for persons who have been granted, or are deemed to have been granted, leave outside the immigration rules made under section 3(2) of the Immigration Act 1971.

items.title: The Redemption of Heritable Securities (Excluded Securities) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order provides that section 11(2) of the Land Tenure Reform (Scotland) Act 1974 will not apply to the heritable securities granted in pursuance of the schemes mentioned in article 2.

items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”), the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (2008 Section) (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”), and the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Transitional and Consequential Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the Transitional Regulations”).

items.title: The Level 1 and Level 2 Disclosure Review Application (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with the procedure for the carrying out of reviews (whether by the Scottish Minsters, the chief constable or the independent reviewer) under Part 1 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) in relation to any of the information included in a Level 1 or Level 2 disclosure.

items.title: The Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 Amendment and Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025 so that provisions in Part 8 (information to be included with certain electronic material at Scottish elections) of the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (“the Act”) will not come into force on 1 October 2025. Instead those provisions, which are listed in Part 1 of the schedule, will come into force on 17 November 2025.

The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 2014 (“the 2014 Order”). The 2014 Order designates sports grounds, classes of sporting events played at those grounds and classes of sporting events outside Great Britain for the purposes of Part II of the Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (sporting events: control of alcohol etc.).

items.title: The Representation of the People (Absent Voting at Local Government Elections) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Representation of the People (Absent Voting at Local Government Elections) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”). The 2007 Regulations apply where a postal or proxy vote is being or has been sought in respect solely of local government elections in Scotland or of a particular local government election in Scotland.

The Rural Support (Simplification and Improvement) (Data Publication) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations simplify and improve the operation of CAP legislation by amending the requirements for publication of the CAP beneficiaries by no longer requiring this to be done on a single UK website.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 5) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Scottish Water, Prevention of Water Pollution (Milngavie Waterworks) Byelaws Extension Order 2025
items.description:
This Order extends the period during which the Scottish Water, Prevention of Water Pollution (Milngavie Waterworks) Byelaws 2015 are to remain in force to 29 September 2026. A copy of the Byelaws may be obtained from Scottish Water, The Bridge, Buchanan Gate Business Park, Cumbernauld Road, Stepps, G33 6FB, and online at 
www.scottishwater.co.uk.

items.title: The Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Amendment Rules 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Rules 2011 (“the principal Rules”).

items.title: The Qualifications Scotland (Appointment of Initial Members) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide that the persons mentioned in the schedule who are, immediately before the member appointment date, members of the Scottish Qualifications Authority, are to be taken to have been appointed as members of Qualifications Scotland under paragraph 2(1) of schedule 1 of the Education (Scotland) Act 2025.

items.title: The Cromarty Firth Port Authority Harbour Revision Order 2025
items.description: This Order defines the harbour limits and confers updated powers on the Cromarty Firth Port Authority, including powers to lease, the making of byelaws and issuing of general and special directions. It also amends or repeals obsolete or otherwise unnecessary statutory provision affecting the port.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Injurious Articles) (Fixed Penalty Notices and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for a fixed penalty notice procedure in relation to offences under the Environmental Protection (Microbeads) (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”), the Environmental Protection (Cotton Buds) (Scotland) Regulations 2019 (“the 2019 Regulations”), the Environmental Protection (Single-use Plastic Products) (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”), and the Environmental Protection (Single-use Vapes) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the making and recovery of charges for health services provided to certain persons who are not ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (Scottish Carbon Budgets) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made under section A4(1) of the Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”) which requires the Scottish Ministers to set Scottish carbon budgets so that every year between 2026 and the net-zero emissions target year is covered by a budget. A Scottish carbon budget is the number of tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent prescribed for a given period. The Scottish Ministers have a duty under section A3 of the 2009 Act to ensure that each Scottish carbon budget target is met.

items.title: The Winter Heating Assistance (Pension Age) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Winter Heating Assistance (Pension Age) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (‘the principal Regulations’).

items.title: The Climate Change (Local Development Plan) (Repeals) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order repeals section 3F of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”) and sections 72 and 73 of the Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”).

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification) Order 2025
items.description: This Order specifies the office holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Scottish Parliament. Part 1 of the schedule specifies the office-holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Parliament. Part 2 of the schedule specifies the office-holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Parliament but only for particular constituencies or regions.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Constituencies and Regions) Order 2025
items.description:
This Order gives effect to the recommendations contained in the Report on the Second Review of Scottish Parliament Boundaries by Boundaries Scotland dated April 2025. The report is available on the Boundaries Scotland website (https://boundaries.scot/) and hard copies can also be requested by telephoning 0131 244 2001 or by emailing 
boundaries.scot@scottishboundaries.gov.uk.

items.title: The A78 Trunk Road (Greenock) (Temporary Prohibition of Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Scottish Parliament Elections (Regional Returning Officers and Constituency Returning Officers) Order 2025
items.description: This Order designates regional returning officers for the purposes of elections to the Scottish Parliament. It also directs the constituency returning officers for those purposes for constituencies situated in more than one local authority area. For constituencies wholly situated in one local authority area, the constituency returning officer is the person who is the returning officer for elections of councillors for that local authority.

items.title: The Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000 (Non-Party Campaigner Code of Practice) (Appointed Date) (Scotland) Order 2025 (revoked)
items.description: A Code of Practice on controlled expenditures made by non-party campaigners during the Scottish devolved regulated period, which precedes a Scottish Parliament election, has been prepared by the Electoral Commission in accordance with section 100AA(1) of the Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000.

items.title: The Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Statutory Guidance on Imprints on Electronic Materials) (Appointed Date) Regulations 2025
items.description: Statutory guidance on information to be included with electronic materials at Scottish elections has been prepared by the Electoral Commission in accordance with section 58(1) of the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025. This guidance concerns the operation of the duties on campaigners under Part 8 of that Act (which requires “imprint” information to be included with electronic campaign material) and the exercise of the Electoral Commission’s functions in relation to breaches or suspected breaches of those duties.

items.title: The Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Saving Provision) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 10, and schedule 3, of the Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”) will expire at the end of 31 March 2025 by virtue of section 11 of the 2022 Act read alongside the Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (Expiry of Section 10: Extension) Regulations 2024. The Rent Adjudication (Temporary Modifications) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”) were made under powers created by schedule 3 of the 2022 Act and will therefore cease to have effect at the end of 31 March 2025.

items.description: These Regulations bring section 6 (effect of plan) of the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the Act”) into force on 16 December 2025.

The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the list of prescribed rural housing bodies in the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Order 2004 to include Tiree Community Development Trust and Woodhaven Housing Company Limited. Rural housing bodies are bodies in favour of which it is competent to create a rural housing burden comprising a right of pre-emption under section 43(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Public Procurement (Agreement on Government Procurement) (Thresholds) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the thresholds that apply in the field of public procurement that are specified in the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015, the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 and the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 respectively in accordance with the obligations of the United Kingdom under the Agreement on Government Procurement agreed among certain World Trade Organisation members including the United Kingdom and as required by each set of Regulations being amended.

items.title: The Disability Assistance (Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance which is a type of disability assistance given by the Scottish Ministers under section 31 of the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018.

items.title: The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the amount which a local authority is to assume that a person will need for their personal requirements per week, in assessing the person’s ability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the 1976 Regulations”) which make provision about the authorisation of motor vehicle competitions and trials, other than trials of speed, on public roads in Scotland.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Intimation and Service) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision so that a requirement in any Act of Sederunt or in the Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 that a document be placed on the walls or doors of civil courts may also be fulfilled by making certain information publicly available on the Scottish Courts and Tribunals Service website.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Electronic Signature and Electronic Transmission of Documents) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for the electronic signature and transmission of documents in civil court proceedings in Scotland.

items.title: The Food (Promotion and Placement) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for restrictions on the promotion and placement of certain foods and drinks that are less healthy.

items.title: The Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 (Commencement) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 31 March 2026 Part 2 of schedule 8 and section 179 (so far as it relates to that part) of the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Tay Crossing to Ballinluig) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Specific Turns, Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Councillors) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a councillor on a Scottish council is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after the day on which they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Members of the House of Commons) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a member of the House of Commons (an “MP”) is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after the day on which they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Members of the House of Lords) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a member of the House of Lords (“a HoL member”) is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the day of the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.description: These Regulations amend the National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”). The 1992 Regulations concern the assessment of a person’s ability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”). By virtue of section 87(3) of the 1968 Act, accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 is to be regarded as accommodation provided under Part III of the National Assistance Act 1948.
